exercises.name: Band Standing Hip Flexion
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor at foot height.

2. Connect the band to your foot with an ankle strap.

3. Stand facing away from the anchor point. Drive the knee of the banded leg up towards your chest.

4. Return to the start position. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Band Standing Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the handles in both hands, with your arms fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull the handles towards your armpits, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat.

exercises.name: Band Standing Y's
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Stand facing the anchor point, hold the handles in both hands, with hands by your thighs.

3. Bring your hands upwards and outwards to form a "Y" position overhead. 

4. Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Straight Arm Pulldown
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point above head height. 

2. With the handles in both hands, stand facing the anchor point. 

3. Reach both arms straight up towards the anchor point, then bring them down towards your sides without bending the elbows. 

4. Reach back up, and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Tricep Kickback
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing on the band with both feet planted roughly shoulder-width apart. Hold the band in both hands next to your hips. 

2. Reach your hips backwards, with knees slightly bent, while maintaining a long spine position.

3. Bend your elbows so they come up to a position next to your ribs. Fully extend your hands backwards, straightening your arms for a moment. 

4. Return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Upright Row
exercises.description:
1) Stand upright with your feet shoulder width apart and the center of an elastic band under your feet. 

2) Grasp the handles so that your palms are facing inward and touching your upper legs with your arms fully extended, elbows slightly bent. 

3) Keeping the handles close to your body, exhale as you raise them straight up until they are just under your chin. 

4) Hold for a moment and then inhale as you reverse the motion back to the starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Band-Assisted GHD Hip Extension
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to the foot pads of the GHD, and looping it around your chest, underneath your arms.

2. With your feet in the foot pads, and facing away from the feet, rest your knees on the large pad.  

3. Fully extend your legs so that your torso is parallel to the floor, with hips in contact with the large pad. 

4. While keeping a neutral spine position, hinge at the hips, bringing your torso down towards the floor. 

5. Using your glutes and hamstrings, press your hips into the large pad, bringing your torso back to the parallel starting position.

exercises.name: Band-Assisted Glute-Ham Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start by wrapping a strong band around the foot pads of the GHD, and looping it around your chest.

2. Have your feet placed in the foot pads, and knees resting on the large pad. 

3. With the upper body in a straight line, and hips locked, bend forward at the knees until your body is parallel to the ground. 

4. In one motion, bring the whole upper body and the hips back up to the start position, with knees directly under shoulders.

exercises.name: Band-Assisted Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.description:
1. Start securing a band to the centre of a pull up bar, and looping it around one of your feet.

2. Grip the pull up bar at around shoulder-width. 

3. Hang with straight arms and straight legs. Lift your feet towards the bar while pressing into the bar with straight arms. 

3. Slowly return to the start position.

exercises.name: Banded "Pull Aparts" (Horizontal Abduction) (3 Ways)
exercises.description: Banded "Pull Aparts" (Horizontal Abduction) (3 Ways)

exercises.name: Banded 2/3 SB squat followed by banded abduction
exercises.description: DO not come all the way up to standing. You want to perform about 2/3 of the lower part of a squat. then go into banded abduction until failure. This is a burn out.

exercises.name: Banded Bear Crawl
exercises.description: With a mini band around your thighs, crawl forward on hands and toes, keeping tension on the band.

exercises.name: Banded glute bridge
exercises.description: Push through heels

exercises.name: Banded Lateral Bear Crawl
exercises.description: In a bear crawl position, move laterally while maintaining tension on the band around your thighs.

exercises.name: Banded Sprinter
exercises.description: Wrap a mini band around your thighs. Assume a sprinter stance and perform fast, alternating knee drives.

exercises.name: Banded swimmer kicks with DB crunch
exercises.description: Stretch up, breathe deep and exhale to engage core. Pull up so that arms are over chest and shoulder blades are pulled down. Pull belly button into core. Begin kicks. If your core starts to flex outward. Rest for a moment and reset.

exercises.name: Banded Thruster
exercises.description: Place a mini band around your thighs. Perform a squat and press overhead, driving knees outward against the band.

exercises.name: Bar Assisted Chin Ups
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing the bar at chest height. Place your hands shoulder-width on the bar. Grip the bar with your palms facing towards you. 

2. Put your feet slightly in front of the bar, and descend down into a squat while keeping your hands on the bar. 

3. Using your arms, pull your body up towards the bar. You can use your single leg to assist yourself as much as you need. 

4. Return down to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Bar Assisted Pull-ups
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing the bar at chest height. Place your hands shoulder-width on the bar. 

2. Put your feet slightly in front of the bar, and descend down into a squat while keeping your hands on the bar. 

3. Using your arms, pull your body up towards the bar. You can use your legs to assist yourself as much as you need. 

4. Return down to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Bar Assisted Single Leg Support Pull-ups
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing the bar at chest height. Place your hands shoulder-width on the bar. 

2. Put your feet slightly in front of the bar, and descend down into a squat while keeping your hands on the bar. Reach one foot out in front, so only one foot is flat on the floor. 

3. Using your arms, pull your body up towards the bar. You can use your single leg to assist yourself as much as you need. 

4. Return down to the start position and repeat. Be sure to alternate feet between sets.

exercises.name: Bar Hang
exercises.description: 1. Grip a pull up bar at about shoulder-width, and hanging with arms and legs fully extended.

exercises.name: Barbell 21
exercises.description:
1. Stand upright and grab a barbell with an underhand grip. Place your hands shoulders-width apart and allow your arms extend toward the floor.

2. Tuck your elbows tight to the sides of your body.

3. Curl upward until you make a 90-degree angle at your elbows. Relax your arms back to full extension and repeat six more curls reaching the 90-degree angle at your elbow.

4. Now, from the 90-degree at your elbow position, curl the weight up until the barbell is one to two inches away from your shoulder.

5. Lower the weight back to the 90-degree elbow position and repeat six more times.

6. Now, allow your arms to return to full extension.

7. This time, curl your arms from full extension all the way to full extension. Keep curling until the bar is about one to two inches away from your shoulder.

8. Repeat six more curls through this full range of motion to complete a total of 21 reps.

exercises.name: Barbell B Stance Hip Thrust
exercises.alternateName: Barbell kick stand, split stance glute bridge
exercises.description:
Set up as you would for a hip thrust, but stagger your feet so one foot is slightly in front of the other (B-stance). 

Drive through the back heel to lift your hips, keeping the barbell steady. Alternate stances.
